danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile sprites in underground facility setting
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring engagement or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct enemy sprites at level endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring tactical weapon use or Heavy Barrel assembly

danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary environmental objects requiring key items
danger_signals.meaning: Resource gating; danger of proceeding without optimal loadout
Resource scarcity increases vulnerability; hazard amplification

safety_signals.visual_form: Crate contents revealing improved weapons and grenades
safety_signals.meaning: Power increase; moment of tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Six visible pieces scattered throughout level
safety_signals.meaning: Progression toward superweapon assembly; major safety reset via one-shot capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Starting player weapon with perpetual ammo
safety_signals.meaning: Baseline survival guarantee; never completely defenseless

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground nuclear missile control complex as operational zone

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types occupying base; enemy leader as final boss
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Seized underground facility; enemy occupation of strategic nuclear asset

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underground facility environment; hostile NPCs throughout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patrols and engagement patterns; base remains locked until infiltration complete

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard weapon and grenade supply UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player vulnerability to boss encounters; reliance on tactical movement and resource management

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Superweapon collected and equipped; visual weapon swap in player sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One-shot enemy elimination capability; 30-second active duration before revert to base weapon; tactical window for boss bypass

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level transitions from Level 1 through final boss encounter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating enemy density and difficulty; boss complexity increases

environmental_storytelling.example: Underground nuclear missile control complex as infiltration objective establishes Cold War espionage scenario without explicit cutscene
Limited Heavy Barrel pieces (three assemblies max per run) implies strategic sacrifice; suggests infiltration is prepared incursion with finite advantages
Final boss encounter signals command structure; defeating leader implies mission objective completion beyond simple survival
Crate-locked improved weapons and grenades suggest base was fortified by occupying force; player must salvage enemy supplies to counter them

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement and fire; requires tactical positioning and grenade economy
One-shot eliminator; wide arc fire; sustained trigger input possible; 30-second duration constraint forces tempo shift
Positional threats requiring evasion or engagement; demonstrate base occupation
Requires Heavy Barrel or sustained standard weapon assault; indicates mission culmination
Immobile until key item collected; visual gating mechanism for progression

notes: Heavy Barrel is a Cold War-era run-and-gun shmup where visual communication relies heavily on: (1) setting authenticity (nuclear facility) to establish stakes; (2) resource visibility (weapon UI, grenade counts) to create tension; (3) superweapon assembly as mechanical narrative—the Heavy Barrel functions as both gameplay tool and thematic centerpiece, with its six-piece distribution creating episodic power spikes that reset player vulnerability. The game communicates political context implicitly: infiltration of seized nuclear installation vs. terrorist occupiers. No explicit faction insignia or color-coded allegiances are documented, suggesting faction identity is conveyed through role (player = infiltrator, enemies = occupiers) and environmental context rather than visual heraldry. Regional differences (Japan vs. USA) noted in sources are copyright/branding only, not content narrative differences.
